chunk_summary: This section focuses on screening and triage methods for identifying and managing undernutrition in infants under six months, referencing WHO guidelines and other authoritative sources.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for identifying severe acute malnutrition (SAM) in infants under six months.
Learn the WHO-recommended screening and triage protocols for malnutrition in this age group.
Recognize the importance of facility-based care for managing severe cases of undernutrition.
Familiarize with global and national guidelines for malnutrition management in infants.
key_facts: Severe acute malnutrition (SAM) in infants under six months is identified using specific anthropometric and clinical criteria.
WHO guidelines emphasize early identification and management to prevent growth and developmental delays.
Facility-based care is recommended for infants with SAM, as outlined in the MoHFW Participant Manual.
Infants at risk of poor growth and development require targeted interventions, as per WHO executive summary tables.
The WHO eLENA tool provides detailed protocols for identifying SAM in infants under six months.
reference_quote: WHO guideline on the prevention and management of wasting and nutritional oedema (acute malnutrition) in infants and children under 5 years — https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240082830
document_key_topics: Screening criteria for SAM in infants under six months
WHO and national guidelines for malnutrition management
